vector_search_builder_workflow.overview: This workflow guides you through setting up and configuring vector databases (Milvus or OpenSearch) for semantic search and RAG applications using IBM Watsonx embeddings.

actions.action: Identify the vector database technology (Milvus or OpenSearch)
Assess scale requirements (document count, query volume)
Understand data sources and ingestion patterns

required_information.item: IBM Cloud Object Storage credentials (if applicable)

phase.name: database_setup
phase.title: Set Up Vector Database

steps.step: Configure vector dimensions to match embedding model
Select distance metric (cosine, L2, dot product)
Set up metadata fields for filtering

milvus_configuration.collection_schema:
from pymilvus import Collection, CollectionSchema, FieldSchema, DataType, connections
# Connect to Milvus connections.connect( alias="default", host=os.getenv("MILVUS_HOST"), port=os.getenv("MILVUS_PORT"), user=os.getenv("MILVUS_USER"), password=os.getenv("MILVUS_PASSWORD"), secure=True )
# Define collection schema fields = [ FieldSchema(name="id", dtype=DataType.INT64, is_primary=True, auto_id=True), FieldSchema(name="embedding", dtype=DataType.FLOAT_VECTOR, dim=768), FieldSchema(name="text", dtype=DataType.VARCHAR, max_length=65535), FieldSchema(name="source", dtype=DataType.VARCHAR, max_length=512), FieldSchema(name="metadata", dtype=DataType.JSON) ]
schema = CollectionSchema(fields, description="Vector search collection") collection = Collection(name="knowledge_base", schema=schema)
# Create HNSW index for optimal performance index_params = { "metric_type": "COSINE", "index_type": "HNSW", "params": { "M": 16, # Number of connections per layer "efConstruction": 256 # Size of dynamic candidate list } } collection.create_index(field_name="embedding", index_params=index_params) collection.load()

phase.name: embedding_configuration
phase.title: Configure Embedding Generation

steps.step: Set up IBM Watsonx embeddings client

phase.watsonx_embeddings_setup:
from ibm_watsonx_ai import Credentials from ibm_watsonx_ai.foundation_models import Embeddings from ibm_watsonx_ai.foundation_models.utils.enums import EmbeddingTypes
# Configure IBM Watsonx credentials credentials = Credentials( url=os.getenv("WATSONX_URL"), api_key=os.getenv("WATSONX_API_KEY") )
# Initialize embeddings model embeddings = Embeddings( model_id=EmbeddingTypes.IBM_SLATE_125M_ENG, # 768 dimensions credentials=credentials, project_id=os.getenv("WATSONX_PROJECT_ID") )
# Test embedding generation test_text = "This is a test document for embedding generation" test_vector = embeddings.embed_query(test_text) print(f"Embedding dimension: {len(test_vector)}")

phase.name: ingestion_pipeline
phase.title: Implement Data Ingestion Pipeline

steps.step: Set up document loaders for source formats
Store vectors with metadata in database
Add progress tracking for large datasets

phase.ingestion_example:
from langchain.text_splitter import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter from langchain.document_loaders import DirectoryLoader import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO) logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
def ingest_documents(source_path: str, collection_name: str): """Ingest documents into vector database"""
# Load documents logger.info(f"Loading documents from {source_path}") loader = DirectoryLoader(source_path, glob="**/*.txt") documents = loader.load() logger.info(f"Loaded {len(documents)} documents")
# Chunk documents splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter( chunk_size=400, chunk_overlap=50, separators=["\n\n", "\n", ". ", " ", ""] ) chunks = splitter.split_documents(documents) logger.info(f"Created {len(chunks)} chunks")
# Generate embeddings in batches batch_size = 100 for i in range(0, len(chunks), batch_size): batch = chunks[i:i+batch_size] texts = [chunk.page_content for chunk in batch]
# Generate embeddings vectors = embeddings.embed_documents(texts)
# Prepare data for insertion data = [ { "embedding": vector, "text": chunk.page_content, "source": chunk.metadata.get("source", ""), "metadata": chunk.metadata } for chunk, vector in zip(batch, vectors) ]
# Insert into Milvus collection.insert(data) logger.info(f"Inserted batch {i//batch_size + 1}/{(len(chunks)-1)//batch_size + 1}")
# Flush to ensure data is persisted collection.flush() logger.info("Ingestion complete")

phase.name: search_implementation
phase.title: Implement Vector Search

steps.step: Implement hybrid search if using OpenSearch

phase.milvus_search:
def search_milvus(query: str, top_k: int = 5, filters: dict = None): """Search Milvus vector database"""
# Generate query embedding query_vector = embeddings.embed_query(query)
# Build filter expression filter_expr = None if filters: filter_expr = " AND ".join([ f'{key} == "{value}"' for key, value in filters.items() ])
# Search search_params = { "metric_type": "COSINE", "params": {"ef": 100} # Search quality parameter }
results = collection.search( data=[query_vector], anns_field="embedding", param=search_params, limit=top_k, expr=filter_expr, output_fields=["text", "source", "metadata"] )
# Format results formatted_results = [] for hits in results: for hit in hits: formatted_results.append({ "text": hit.entity.get("text"), "source": hit.entity.get("source"), "metadata": hit.entity.get("metadata"), "score": hit.score })
return formatted_results

phase.name: testing_validation
phase.title: Test and Validate Setup
phase.validation_script:
def validate_vector_search_setup(): """Comprehensive validation of vector search setup"""
print("=== Vector Search Setup Validation ===\n")
# 1. Test database connection print("1. Testing database connection...") try: collection.load() print("✓ Database connection successful") except Exception as e: print(f"✗ Database connection failed: {e}") return False
# 2. Verify collection schema print("\n2. Verifying collection schema...") schema = collection.schema print(f"✓ Collection: {collection.name}") print(f"✓ Fields: {[field.name for field in schema.fields]}")
# 3. Test embedding generation print("\n3. Testing embedding generation...") test_text = "This is a test query" test_vector = embeddings.embed_query(test_text) print(f"✓ Generated embedding with {len(test_vector)} dimensions")
# 4. Test search print("\n4. Testing search functionality...") results = search_milvus(test_text, top_k=3) print(f"✓ Search returned {len(results)} results")
# 5. Measure latency print("\n5. Measuring search latency...") import time start = time.time() for _ in range(10): search_milvus(test_text, top_k=5) avg_latency = (time.time() - start) / 10 print(f"✓ Average search latency: {avg_latency*1000:.2f}ms")
print("\n=== Validation Complete ===") return True

optimization.name: index_tuning
optimization.description: Optimize HNSW index parameters for your workload

parameters.param: Number of connections per layer (8-64, default 16)

guidelines.guideline: Higher M = better recall, more memory
Higher efConstruction = better index quality, slower build
Higher ef = better recall, slower search

optimization.name: batch_processing
optimization.description: Process embeddings in batches for efficiency

best_practices.practice: Use batch size of 100-500 for embedding generation
Insert data in batches to reduce network overhead
Implement parallel processing for large datasets

completion_criteria.criterion: Vector database is connected and accessible
Collection/index is created with correct schema
Embedding generation is working with IBM Watsonx
All code is documented and tested
